phases.name: Fix Section Order screen to include newly-added staple sections
phases.description: Ship the P0 defect fix (wire appendNewSections into the read-time merge so newly-added sections appear in the Section Order settings screen when a custom order is saved) followed by the P2 reactivity hardening (settings screen reflects staple-library mutations while mounted, not only on re-mount). Each step introduces a regression test that fails against current code and passes after the fix. Scope per RCA sections 5.1 and 5.2 and scope.md.

steps.name: Wire appendNewSections into SectionOrderSettingsScreen orderedEntries (P0)
steps.description: Add a regression test that seeds a saved custom section order, adds a staple introducing a new section key, renders SectionOrderSettingsScreen, and asserts the new section appears as the last row (after the existing custom-ordered sections). The test must fail against current code and pass after the fix. Fix: modify the orderedEntries useMemo in src/ui/SectionOrderSettingsScreen.tsx so that when order !== null it calls appendNewSections(order, knownSectionKeys) and maps the merged array through parseSectionKey. Read-time merge only — do NOT call reorder/saveOrder from the screen. Do not modify src/domain/section-ordering.ts, src/ports/section-order-storage.ts, or any hook. Reuses the already-tested pure function from RCA evidence 1 (src/domain/section-ordering.ts:25-32).
steps.criteria: Given a custom section order is saved (for example ['Deli::null','Dairy::3','Bakery::1']) and the staple library already contains staples covering those sections, when a new staple whose storeLocation introduces section key 'Sushi Bar::null' is added and then SectionOrderSettingsScreen is rendered, then the rendered section rows appear in the order Deli, Dairy, Bakery, Sushi Bar (new section appended to the end). The existing reset-to-default flow still clears the custom order and falls back to staple-derived sections. All pre-existing regression tests in tests/regression/ui/section-order-settings.test.tsx continue to pass. No writes to sectionOrderStorage occur as a side effect of opening the screen.

steps.name: Make known section keys reactive to staple-library mutations (P2)
steps.description: Add a regression test that renders SectionOrderSettingsScreen with a saved custom order, then — while the screen stays mounted — mutates the staple library by adding a staple introducing a new section, and asserts the rendered row list updates to include the new section without unmounting or re-navigating. The test must fail against code that only has step 01-01 applied (because knownSectionKeys is memoised against a stable stapleLibrary reference per RCA evidence 4) and pass after the fix. Fix (crafter chooses the cleaner of the two functional options during GREEN): (1) inject a live staples array from an existing reactive hook such as useStaples() into the settings screen so knownSectionKeys derives from a React state-backed array; or (2) add a minimal functional subscription on the staple-library factory (stapleLibrary.subscribe(listener)) and have the hook or screen subscribe. Prefer option 1 (UI-local reactivity) if a reactive staples hook already exists. Do NOT introduce Firestore changes, port-interface changes, or cross-device sync logic. If option 2 requires a domain-level subscription that touches src/domain/** or src/ports/**, flag in wave-decisions before proceeding.
steps.criteria: Given SectionOrderSettingsScreen is rendered and currently mounted with a saved custom order, when a staple whose storeLocation introduces a new section key is added to the staple library (while the screen remains mounted, no navigation), then the rendered section row list updates within the next render cycle to include a row for the new section appended after the existing custom-ordered sections. The reset-to-default behaviour is unchanged. The P0 behaviour established in step 01-01 (new sections appear on re-mount with a saved custom order) continues to hold. No changes are made to files under src/ports/** or src/domain/** unless wave-decisions has recorded an explicit deviation.
